initiative_name: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted adaptation guidance
parent_statement: Action 3.7: Deliver a rolling programme of targeted, non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers to assess and manage climate risks.
coverage_assessment: The sub-actions broadly cover the parent goal of delivering a rolling programme of non-statutory adaptation guidance for decision makers: they span climate projections, policy-making methodology, DAPP, scenario use, local government decision support, mātauranga Māori integration, adaptation plan preparation, and local risk assessment processes. A notable gap is the absence of explicit delivery milestones (e.g., publication dates) for most guidance products, which makes it hard to verify the “rolling programme” cadence beyond one six-yearly cycle item.
coherence_assessment: The sub-actions are complementary and non-contradictory: they form a logical suite of guidance documents that together support risk assessment and adaptation planning across government and stakeholders. There is some redundancy (multiple generic “produce guidance” items) but they address distinct audiences/tools (projections, DAPP, scenarios, standard plans, local risk assessments).
weak_signals: non-statutory guidance, promote, assists/supports/enables language, no explicit publication deadlines for 7 of 8 sub-actions, few/no quantifiable outcome metrics (uptake/coverage/compliance)
strong_signals: clear tangible deliverables (produce guidance / update guides), explicit six-yearly update cycle tied to national risk assessments, covers multiple decision-maker audiences (central/local government, businesses, communities)
initiative_reasoning: 1) The initiative aims to roll out an ongoing programme of non-statutory adaptation guidance to help decision makers assess and manage climate risks, with a note that it could support future regulatory requirements via the National Planning Framework. 2) Sub-action strength mix: 1 strong (the six-yearly update cycle for the local risk assessment guide), 6 moderate (clear deliverable: producing/updating specific guidance documents, but no dates/metrics), and 1 weak (promoting use of projections guidance without a deadline or measurable uptake). 3) The parent provides programmatic context (a ‘programme of non-statutory guidance’) and hints at linkage to future regulation (‘could also support regulatory requirements’), but it does not establish a binding mechanism or a defined spatial scope; thus it only modestly lifts weaker subs. 4) Coverage is good (0.75) because the set spans key tools and audiences for adaptation planning and risk assessment; coherence is high (0.85) because the guidance products are complementary and logically aligned. 5) Applying the initiative soundness rule: it fails SOUND because aggregate_measurability is No (few timelines/targets) even though coverage/coherence are strong and at least one sub-action is strong. It qualifies as PARTIAL because coverage_score >= 0.4, there are multiple moderate/strong sub-actions, and there is at least one explicit timeline/cycle (six-yearly) in the cluster. Final verdict is True because PARTIAL with confidence_score (0.6) >= 0.55.
